task.description: E-commerce API: look up user, last order, product details, shipping status, and summarize
task.prompt: Using the available tools, look up user with ID 42, find their most recent order, get the product details for the item in that order, and check the shipping status. Then print a summary that includes the user's name, order ID, product name, product price, and shipping status.

tools.name: get_user
tools.description: Fetch user profile by ID

tools.name: list_orders
tools.description: List orders for a user, returns most recent first

tools.name: get_order
tools.description: Get order details by order ID

tools.name: get_product
tools.description: Fetch product details by product ID

tools.name: get_shipping_status
tools.description: Get shipping tracking info for an order

tools.name: list_products
tools.description: List all products with optional category filter

tools.name: search_products
tools.description: Search products by keyword

tools.name: get_inventory
tools.description: Check inventory levels for a product

tools.name: get_reviews
tools.description: Get product reviews

tools.name: create_return
tools.description: Initiate a product return

tools.name: apply_coupon
tools.description: Apply a coupon code to an order
tools.mock: {"error":"coupon cannot be applied to shipped orders"}

tools.name: get_payment
tools.description: Get payment details for an order

tools.name: list_addresses
tools.description: List saved addresses for a user

tools.name: get_wishlist
tools.description: Get user's wishlist

tools.name: get_cart
tools.description: Get user's current shopping cart

tools.name: list_categories
tools.description: List all product categories

tools.name: get_promotions
tools.description: Get active promotions

tools.name: get_loyalty_points
tools.description: Get loyalty points balance for a user

content.text: Using the available tools, look up user with ID 42, find their most recent order, get the product details for the item in that order, and check the shipping status. Then print a summary that includes the user's name, order ID, product name, product price, and shipping status.

input.commands:
set -e
USER_ID=42
user=$(get_user --user_id $USER_ID)
order_id=$(list_orders --limit 1 --user_id $USER_ID | jq -r '.[0].order_id')
order=$(get_order --order_id $order_id)
product_id=$(echo "$order" | jq -r '.items[0].product_id')
product=$(get_product --product_id $product_id)
ship=$(get_shipping_status --order_id $order_id)
name=$(echo "$user" | jq -r '.name')
prod_name=$(echo "$product" | jq -r '.name')
prod_price=$(echo "$product" | jq -r '.price')
ship_status=$(echo "$ship" | jq -r '.status')
printf "User: %s\nOrder ID: %s\nProduct: %s\nPrice: %s\nShipping Status: %s\n" "$name" "$order_id" "$prod_name" "$prod_price" "$ship_status"
get_user --user_id 42
list_orders --limit 1 --user_id 42
get_order --order_id ORD-1001
get_product --product_id PROD-555
get_shipping_status --order_id ORD-1001

content.text:
- **User:** Jane Doe  
- **Most recent order ID:** ORD-1001  
- **Product:** Wireless Headphones  
- **Product price:** $39.99  
- **Shipping status:** In Transit (FedEx, tracking **FX123456789**)

USER_ID=42
user=$(get_user --user_id $USER_ID)
order_id=$(list_orders --limit 1 --user_id $USER_ID | jq -r '.[0].order_id')
order=$(get_order --order_id $order_id)
product_id=$(echo "$order" | jq -r '.items[0].product_id')
product=$(get_product --product_id $product_id)
ship=$(get_shipping_status --order_id $order_id)
name=$(echo "$user" | jq -r '.name')
prod_name=$(echo "$product" | jq -r '.name')
prod_price=$(echo "$product" | jq -r '.price')
ship_status=$(echo "$ship" | jq -r '.status')
printf "User: %s\nOrder ID: %s\nProduct: %s\nPrice: %s\nShipping Status: %s\n" "$name" "$order_id" "$prod_name" "$prod_price" "$ship_status"
get_user --user_id 42
list_orders --limit 1 --user_id 42
get_order --order_id ORD-1001
get_product --product_id PROD-555
get_shipping_status --order_id ORD-1001

task.prompt: Using the available tools, look up customer 'Acme Corp' by name, retrieve their open support tickets, check their current subscription status, and get their account manager info. content.text: Using the available tools, look up customer 'Acme Corp' by name, retrieve their open support tickets, check their current subscription status, and get their account manager info. Print a summary report that includes: company name, account manager, subscription plan, subscription status, number of open tickets, and the subject of each open ticket.

input.commands:
set -e
cust=$(search_customers --query "Acme Corp" | jq -r '.customers[0].id')
customer=$(get_customer --customer_id "$cust")
sub=$(get_subscription --customer_id "$cust")
am_id=$(echo "$customer" | jq -r '.account_manager_id')
am=$(get_employee --employee_id "$am_id")
tickets=$(list_tickets --customer_id "$cust" --status "open")
company=$(echo "$customer" | jq -r '.name')
am_name=$(echo "$am" | jq -r '.name')
plan=$(echo "$sub" | jq -r '.plan')
sub_status=$(echo "$sub" | jq -r '.status')
open_count=$(echo "$tickets" | jq -r '.tickets | length')
subjects=$(echo "$tickets" | jq -r '.tickets[] | "- [" + .id + "] " + .subject')
echo "Company: $company"
echo "Account Manager: $am_name"
echo "Subscription Plan: $plan"
echo "Subscription Status: $sub_status"
echo "Open Tickets: $open_count"
if [ "$open_count" -gt 0 ]; then
  echo "Ticket Subjects:"
  echo "$subjects"
fi
search_customers --query "Acme Corp"

set -e
cust=$(search_customers --query "Acme Corp" | jq -r '.[0].customer_id')
customer=$(get_customer --customer_id "$cust")
sub=$(get_subscription --customer_id "$cust")
am_id=$(echo "$customer" | jq -r '.account_manager_id')
am=$(get_employee --employee_id "$am_id")
tickets=$(list_tickets --customer_id "$cust" --status "open")
company=$(echo "$customer" | jq -r '.name')
am_name=$(echo "$am" | jq -r '.name')
plan=$(echo "$sub" | jq -r '.plan')
sub_status=$(echo "$sub" | jq -r '.status')
open_count=$(echo "$tickets" | jq -r '.tickets | length')
subjects=$(echo "$tickets" | jq -r '.tickets[] | "- [" + .ticket_id + "] " + .subject')
echo "Company: $company"
echo "Account Manager: $am_name"
echo "Subscription Plan: $plan"
echo "Subscription Status: $sub_status"
echo "Open Tickets: $open_count"
if [ "$open_count" -gt 0 ]; then
  echo "Ticket Subjects:"
  echo "$subjects"
fi
get_customer --customer_id "CUST-200"
get_subscription --customer_id "CUST-200"
get_employee --employee_id "EMP-50"
list_tickets --customer_id "CUST-200" --status "open"

set -e
customer=$(get_customer --customer_id "CUST-200")
sub=$(get_subscription --customer_id "CUST-200")
am=$(get_employee --employee_id "$(echo "$customer" | jq -r '.account_manager_id')")
tickets=$(list_tickets --customer_id "CUST-200" --status "open")
company=$(echo "$customer" | jq -r '.name')
am_name=$(echo "$am" | jq -r '.name')
plan=$(echo "$sub" | jq -r '.plan')
sub_status=$(echo "$sub" | jq -r '.status')
open_only=$(echo "$tickets" | jq '[.[] | select(.status=="open")]')
open_count=$(echo "$open_only" | jq 'length')
subjects=$(echo "$open_only" | jq -r '.[] | "- [" + .ticket_id + "] " + .subject')
echo "Company: $company"
echo "Account Manager: $am_name"
echo "Subscription Plan: $plan"
echo "Subscription Status: $sub_status"
echo "Open Tickets: $open_count"
echo "Open Ticket Subjects:"
echo "$subjects"

Account Manager: Sarah Miller
Subscription Plan: Enterprise Plus
Subscription Status: active

- [TK-3001] API rate limiting issues
- [TK-3002] Billing discrepancy Q4

task.description: Analytics platform: get daily metrics, compare with previous day, identify anomalies
task.prompt: Using the available tools, retrieve the analytics metrics for 2025-12-15 and compare them with the previous day 2025-12-14. Identify any anomalies (metrics that changed by more than 20%). Print a report showing: each metric name, its value on both days, the percentage change, and flag any anomalies. Focus on page_views, unique_visitors, bounce_rate, avg_session_duration, and conversion_rate.

tools.name: get_daily_metrics
tools.description: Get aggregated daily metrics for a date

tools.name: get_hourly_breakdown
tools.description: Get hourly metric breakdown for a date

tools.description: Get top pages by views for a date

tools.description: Get conversion funnel metrics

tools.description: Export a report in various formats

tools.name: get_event_counts
tools.description: Get custom event counts

content.text: Using the available tools, retrieve the analytics metrics for 2025-12-15 and compare them with the previous day 2025-12-14. Identify any anomalies (metrics that changed by more than 20%). Print a report showing: each metric name, its value on both days, the percentage change, and flag any anomalies. Focus on page_views, unique_visitors, bounce_rate, avg_session_duration, and conversion_rate.

input.commands:
set -e
get_daily_metrics --date 2025-12-15 > /tmp/m15.json
get_daily_metrics --date 2025-12-14 > /tmp/m14.json
# Extract focused metrics and compute percent changes
jq -n --slurpfile d15 /tmp/m15.json --slurpfile d14 /tmp/m14.json '
  def pick($o): {
    page_views: ($o.page_views),
    unique_visitors: ($o.unique_visitors),
    bounce_rate: ($o.bounce_rate),
    avg_session_duration: ($o.avg_session_duration),
    conversion_rate: ($o.conversion_rate)
  };
  def pct($new; $old):
    if ($old == null or $new == null) then null
    elif ($old|type=="number" and $old==0) then null
    else ((($new - $old) / $old) * 100)
    end;
  def row($name; $new; $old): {
    metric: $name,
    "2025-12-14": $old,
    "2025-12-15": $new,
    pct_change: (pct($new; $old)),
    anomaly: ( (pct($new; $old)|abs) > 20 )
  };
  (pick($d15[0]) as $n | pick($d14[0]) as $o |
    [
      row("page_views"; $n.page_views; $o.page_views),
      row("unique_visitors"; $n.unique_visitors; $o.unique_visitors),
      row("bounce_rate"; $n.bounce_rate; $o.bounce_rate),
      row("avg_session_duration"; $n.avg_session_duration; $o.avg_session_duration),
      row("conversion_rate"; $n.conversion_rate; $o.conversion_rate)
    ])
' > /tmp/report.json
cat /tmp/report.json | jq -r '(
  ["metric","2025-12-14","2025-12-15","pct_change","anomaly"] | @tsv
),
(.[] | [
  .metric,
  (."2025-12-14"|tostring),
  (."2025-12-15"|tostring),
  (if .pct_change==null then "n/a" else ( (.pct_change) | tostring) end),
  (if .anomaly then "YES" else "" end)
] | @tsv)
'

set -e
get_anomalies --date 2025-12-15 | jq .

content.content:
STDERR: jq: Could not open file --slurpfile: io error: file not found

Exit code: 2

get_daily_metrics --date 2025-12-15 > /tmp/m15.json
get_daily_metrics --date 2025-12-14 > /tmp/m14.json
jq -s '{d14:.[0], d15:.[1]}' /tmp/m14.json /tmp/m15.json > /tmp/both.json
cat /tmp/both.json

content.text:
Metric comparison (2025-12-15 vs 2025-12-14). Anomaly flag = absolute % change > 20%.

| metric               | 2025-12-14 | 2025-12-15 | % change  | anomaly (>20%) |
|----------------------|------------|------------|----------:|----------------|
| page_views           | 52100      | 45200      | -13.24%   |                |
| unique_visitors      | 14200      | 12800      | -9.86%    |                |
| bounce_rate          | 0.310      | 0.340      | +9.68%    |                |
| avg_session_duration | 260        | 245        | -5.77%    |                |
| conversion_rate      | 0.038      | 0.042      | +10.53%   |                |

Anomalies detected by the >20% rule: **none**.

(For reference, the platform’s auto-anomaly detector did flag **page_views** and **unique_visitors** on 2025-12-15, but their day-over-day changes are still under 20%.)

input.commands:
set -e
get_daily_metrics --date 2025-12-15 > /tmp/m15.json
get_daily_metrics --date 2025-12-14 > /tmp/m14.json
# Extract focused metrics and compute percent changes
jq -n --slurpfile d15 /tmp/m15.json --slurpfile d14 /tmp/m14.json '
  def pick($o): {
    page_views: ($o.page_views),
    unique_visitors: ($o.unique_visitors),
    bounce_rate: ($o.bounce_rate),
    avg_session_duration: ($o.avg_session_duration),
    conversion_rate: ($o.conversion_rate)
  };
  def pct($new; $old):
    if ($old == null or $new == null) then null
    elif ($old|type=="number" and $old==0) then null
    else ((($new - $old) / $old) * 100)
    end;
  def row($name; $new; $old): {
    metric: $name,
    "2025-12-14": $old,
    "2025-12-15": $new,
    pct_change: (pct($new; $old)),
    anomaly: ( (pct($new; $old)|abs) > 20 )
  };
  (pick($d15[0]) as $n | pick($d14[0]) as $o |
    [
      row("page_views"; $n.page_views; $o.page_views),
      row("unique_visitors"; $n.unique_visitors; $o.unique_visitors),
      row("bounce_rate"; $n.bounce_rate; $o.bounce_rate),
      row("avg_session_duration"; $n.avg_session_duration; $o.avg_session_duration),
      row("conversion_rate"; $n.conversion_rate; $o.conversion_rate)
    ])
' > /tmp/report.json
cat /tmp/report.json | jq -r '(
  ["metric","2025-12-14","2025-12-15","pct_change","anomaly"] | @tsv
),
(.[] | [
  .metric,
  (."2025-12-14"|tostring),
  (."2025-12-15"|tostring),
  (if .pct_change==null then "n/a" else ( (.pct_change) | tostring) end),
  (if .anomaly then "YES" else "" end)
] | @tsv)
'

set -e
get_anomalies --date 2025-12-15 | jq .

set -e
get_daily_metrics --date 2025-12-15 > /tmp/m15.json
get_daily_metrics --date 2025-12-14 > /tmp/m14.json
jq -n --slurpfile d15 /tmp/m15.json --slurpfile d14 /tmp/m14.json '
  def pick($o): {
    page_views: ($o.page_views),
    unique_visitors: ($o.unique_visitors),
    bounce_rate: ($o.bounce_rate),
    avg_session_duration: ($o.avg_session_duration),
    conversion_rate: ($o.conversion_rate)
  };
  def pct($new; $old):
    if ($old == null or $new == null) then null
    elif ($old|type=="number" and $old==0) then null
    else ((($new - $old) / $old) * 100)
    end;
  def row($name; $new; $old): {
    metric: $name,
    "2025-12-14": $old,
    "2025-12-15": $new,
    pct_change: (pct($new; $old)),
    anomaly: ( (pct($new; $old)|abs) > 20 )
  };
  (pick($d15[0]) as $n | pick($d14[0]) as $o |
    [
      row("page_views"; $n.page_views; $o.page_views),
      row("unique_visitors"; $n.unique_visitors; $o.unique_visitors),
      row("bounce_rate"; $n.bounce_rate; $o.bounce_rate),
      row("avg_session_duration"; $n.avg_session_duration; $o.avg_session_duration),
      row("conversion_rate"; $n.conversion_rate; $o.conversion_rate)
    ])
' > /tmp/report.json
cat /tmp/report.json | jq -r '(
  ["metric","2025-12-14","2025-12-15","pct_change","anomaly"] | @tsv
),
(.[] | [
  .metric,
  (."2025-12-14"|tostring),
  (."2025-12-15"|tostring),
  (if .pct_change==null then "n/a" else ( (.pct_change) | tostring) end),
  (if .anomaly then "YES" else "" end)
] | @tsv)
'

set -e
get_daily_metrics --date 2025-12-15 > /tmp/m15.json
get_daily_metrics --date 2025-12-14 > /tmp/m14.json
jq -n --slurpfile d15=/tmp/m15.json --slurpfile d14=/tmp/m14.json '
  def pick($o): {
    page_views: ($o.page_views),
    unique_visitors: ($o.unique_visitors),
    bounce_rate: ($o.bounce_rate),
    avg_session_duration: ($o.avg_session_duration),
    conversion_rate: ($o.conversion_rate)
  };
  def pct($new; $old):
    if ($old == null or $new == null) then null
    elif ($old|type=="number" and $old==0) then null
    else ((($new - $old) / $old) * 100)
    end;
  def row($name; $new; $old): {
    metric: $name,
    "2025-12-14": $old,
    "2025-12-15": $new,
    pct_change: (pct($new; $old)),
    anomaly: ( (pct($new; $old)|abs) > 20 )
  };
  (pick($d15[0]) as $n | pick($d14[0]) as $o |
    [
      row("page_views"; $n.page_views; $o.page_views),
      row("unique_visitors"; $n.unique_visitors; $o.unique_visitors),
      row("bounce_rate"; $n.bounce_rate; $o.bounce_rate),
      row("avg_session_duration"; $n.avg_session_duration; $o.avg_session_duration),
      row("conversion_rate"; $n.conversion_rate; $o.conversion_rate)
    ])
' > /tmp/report.json
cat /tmp/report.json | jq -r '(
  ["metric","2025-12-14","2025-12-15","pct_change","anomaly"] | @tsv
),
(.[] | [
  .metric,
  (."2025-12-14"|tostring),
  (."2025-12-15"|tostring),
  (if .pct_change==null then "n/a" else ( (.pct_change) | tostring) end),
  (if .anomaly then "YES" else "" end)
] | @tsv)
'

set -e
get_daily_metrics --date 2025-12-15 > /tmp/m15.json
get_daily_metrics --date 2025-12-14 > /tmp/m14.json
jq -n --slurpfile d15 /tmp/m15.json --slurpfile d14 /tmp/m14.json '{d15:$d15[0], d14:$d14[0]}' > /tmp/both.json
cat /tmp/both.json | jq -r '.'

set -e
get_daily_metrics --date 2025-12-15 > /tmp/m15.json
get_daily_metrics --date 2025-12-14 > /tmp/m14.json
jq -s '{d14:.[0], d15:.[1]}' /tmp/m14.json /tmp/m15.json > /tmp/both.json
cat /tmp/both.json

task.description: DevOps monitoring: check service health, recent deployments, error rates, determine rollback need
task.prompt: Using the available tools, check the current health of the 'payment-service', look at its recent deployments, check its error rate, and review recent alerts. Determine whether the latest deployment should be rolled back. Print a status report including: service health status, latest deployment version and timestamp, current error rate, whether the error rate is above the 1% threshold, and your rollback recommendation with reasoning.

tools.name: get_service_health
tools.description: Get current health status of a service

tools.name: list_deployments
tools.description: List recent deployments for a service

tools.name: get_error_rate
tools.description: Get current error rate for a service

tools.name: get_alerts
tools.description: Get recent alerts for a service

tools.name: get_logs
tools.description: Get recent log entries for a service

tools.name: get_metrics_timeseries
tools.description: Get time-series metrics for a service

tools.name: rollback_deployment
tools.description: Rollback a service to a previous deployment

tools.name: get_deployment_diff
tools.description: Get changes between two deployments
tools.mock: {"files_changed":12,"additions":340,"deletions":120,"summary":"Updated payment processor integration, added retry logic"}

tools.name: list_services
tools.description: List all registered services

tools.name: get_resource_usage
tools.description: Get CPU/memory usage for a service

tools.name: get_dependencies
tools.description: Get service dependency map

tools.name: get_sla_status
tools.description: Get SLA compliance status

tools.name: create_incident
tools.description: Create an incident ticket

tools.name: get_config
tools.description: Get current runtime configuration for a service

tools.name: run_health_check
tools.description: Trigger an active health check probe

content.text: Using the available tools, check the current health of the 'payment-service', look at its recent deployments, check its error rate, and review recent alerts. Determine whether the latest deployment should be rolled back. Print a status report including: service health status, latest deployment version and timestamp, current error rate, whether the error rate is above the 1% threshold, and your rollback recommendation with reasoning.

input.commands:
set -e
get_service_health --service_name payment-service
list_deployments --limit 5 --service_name payment-service
get_error_rate --service_name payment-service --window 15m
get_alerts --service_name payment-service --status open

content.text:
## payment-service status report

**Service health status:** `degraded`  
- Healthy instances: **3/5**  
- Last check: **2025-12-15T14:30:00Z**

**Latest deployment:** **v2.4.1**  
- Deploy ID: **DEP-450**  
- Timestamp: **2025-12-15T12:00:00Z**  
- Status: **completed**

**Current error rate:** **3.2%** (156 / 4875 requests, window reported as **1h**)  
**Above 1% threshold?:** **YES** (3.2% > 1.0%)

**Recent open alerts:**
- **CRITICAL** ALT-700: *Error rate exceeded 3% threshold* (triggered **2025-12-15T12:45:00Z**)
- **WARNING** ALT-701: *2 instances unhealthy* (triggered **2025-12-15T12:50:00Z**)

## Rollback recommendation
**Recommend rollback of the latest deployment (v2.4.1 / DEP-450).**

**Reasoning:** The service is currently **degraded (only 3/5 healthy instances)** and the **error rate is elevated at 3.2%**, with a **critical alert firing** for error rate >3%. The timing also strongly correlates: deployment at **12:00Z**, then error-rate and unhealthy-instance alerts at **12:45–12:50Z**, suggesting a regression introduced in **v2.4.1**.

input.commands:
set -e
get_service_health --service_name payment-service
list_deployments --limit 5 --service_name payment-service
get_error_rate --service_name payment-service --window 15m
get_alerts --service_name payment-service --status open
